name: Tap-through
title: QR landing
summary: A mobile-first landing a printed QR code points to — a brand header, one featured product with a tap-to-copy promo code, a single primary call-to-action, secondary links, and a QR code the page renders itself.
setup_questions: What does the QR live on (packaging, poster, table tent, sticker) and what's the "scan to…" framing it should open with?
What's the single action you most want the visitor to take (reorder, book, claim a discount, follow, sign up)?
What's the brand name, the one product or promo to feature (with a price or offer), and the accent color?
Optional - give 3 to 5 secondary links (menu, brew guide, story, hours, Instagram) plus your address and contact.
photo_terms: small batch coffee roaster bag copper label
phone scanning qr code on a cafe table
sections: Brand header with logo mark and city locator
'You scanned this' intro with scan-to framing and headline
One product / promo card with photo, tasting notes, price, and a tap-to-copy discount code
Secondary links list (brew guide, story, cafe, social, wholesale)
Self-rendered QR code pointing at the page's own URL, with a copy-link button
Footer with address, hours, and contact
based_on: The mobile landing a printed QR code points to — the 'linktree-for-a-product' / 'scan-to-reorder' pattern used by coffee bags, restaurant table tents, packaging inserts, and event posters. Modeled here as a Portland coffee roaster's reorder page.
